experiment.name: prompt-tuning-classifier
experiment.question: Does a cross-source prompt outperform the baseline classifier on a B2B SaaS transaction-categorization task?
experiment.metric: Classification accuracy vs human-labeled ground truth (judge: Claude Sonnet 4.6)
experiment.threshold_rule: Aggregate effect >= +5pp AND no per-slice loss > 2pp (CI lower bound)

verdict.rationale: v2 passes the aggregate threshold (+8.9pp, CI lower bound +5.0pp == registered threshold) AND both per-slice rules (Enterprise +13.4pp, SMB +6.7pp; both CI lower bounds positive). v3 also passes aggregate but FAILS the per-slice rule on SMB (-3.3pp, CI [-8.8pp, +2.2pp] crosses both zero and the -2pp loss floor). Aggregate winners are not winners when a major slice regresses. Ship v2; kill v3.
